About

Where planning meets data science

I'm a Senior Demand Planner based in Barcelona with a rare combination: deep operational experience in S&OP, SAP IBP, and cross-market forecasting — plus hands-on data science skills from my Le Wagon AI Bootcamp.

I've spent my career building the tools that make planning faster and smarter: statistical forecasting models, automated reporting pipelines, executive dashboards, and AI-assisted S&OP commentary. From sophisticated Excel tools and Google Apps Scripts at Diageo and PUIG to Python pipelines and Looker at Too Good To Go — I build what the role needs.

I've forecasted across UK, Ireland, France, Spain, Italy, and global gifting markets, and led S&OP cycles presenting directly to senior leadership across commercial, procurement, and finance.

Selected Work

Dashboards & Analysis

A selection of dashboards and analytical models built across my career. All data anonymised.

PUIG · Barcelona

New Product Launch Tracking Dashboard

Executive-level Power BI dashboard tracking fragrance launches across Prestige, Lifestyle, and Niche portfolios in Spain. Built across 6 tabs — Executive Summary, SKU Follow Up, Customer Launches, Launches Gantt, and Comparativos.

Covers pipeline vs actuals, brand-level sell-through %, SKU-level detail down to individual retailer (Sephora, El Corte Inglés, Douglas, FNAC), monthly sell-through curves, and replenishment tracking. Colour-coded pipeline achievement used weekly by commercial, supply, and finance teams across 2021–2025.

Power BI SAP IBP Launch Planning S&OP Retail Analytics Executive Reporting
Too Good To Go · Barcelona

Missed Opportunities vs Sales Analysis

Python analysis identifying months where supply constraints capped actual sales below predicted demand — quantifying missed revenue opportunities and informing partner stock alignment strategy.

PythonmatplotlibDemand ForecastingSupply Planning
Too Good To Go · Barcelona

Demand Driver Correlation Analysis

Feature correlation analysis across 20+ demand metrics to identify the strongest predictors of sales. Findings directly fed into the forecast model's feature selection and informed S&OP commercial assumptions.

PythonML · RegressionFeature EngineeringPandas
Too Good To Go · Barcelona

Net Sales vs Marketing Spend — UK & USA

Cross-market commercial analytics tracking Net Sales vs Marketing Spend across UK and USA — revealing marketing ROI patterns and feeding directly into S&OP commercial assumptions.

PythonmatplotlibCommercial AnalyticsMarketing ROI
Too Good To Go · Barcelona

Demand Forecast Case Study — Ridge Regression Model

End-to-end demand forecasting case study — data validation, feature engineering, and a Ridge Regression model delivering highly accurate forecasts used for S&OP planning and year-end volume projections.

PythonRegressionTime SeriesS&OP
PUIG · Barcelona

Calendar Year Sales Dashboard

Power BI report showing sales by SKU, store, and channel (Online/Offline) for 2024–2025, with YTD vs LYTD % change and revenue by product group. Used for monthly commercial review and channel mix tracking.

Code & Automation

Automation Pipelines

Beyond analysis, I build the infrastructure that keeps data flowing automatically — eliminating manual exports, consolidating multi-market data, and keeping planning tools always up to date.

Python · Google Colab
Multi-Market Daily Sales Aggregation
Too Good To Go · Barcelona

Python pipeline running in Google Colab that pulls Sales Orders data from 16 Google Sheets across 11 countries, handles currency conversion (DKK and PLN to EUR), cleans and aggregates 3.9M+ rows, and writes a consolidated Excel output to a shared planning drive — fully automated, no manual export required.

3.9M
Rows processed
11
Countries
16
Source sheets
# Pull Sales Orders from 16 sheets across 11 markets for url in sheet_urls: df = get_as_dataframe(gc.open_by_url(url).sheet1) # Currency conversion: DKK → EUR, PLN → EUR if country == "DK": df["revenue"] /= 7.45 combined = pd.concat(all_dfs) # 3,991,617 rows
Google Apps Script
GMS Production Data Auto-Import
Too Good To Go · Barcelona

Apps Script that automatically detects the latest GMS production CSV file in a shared Drive folder (by filename date pattern), parses it, deduplicates against existing data using composite keys (country + date + SKU), and appends only new rows — keeping the production planning sheet always current without any manual intervention.

0
Manual steps
Auto
Deduplication
Daily
Frequency
// Find latest GMSLite26_MonthDD.csv, skip duplicates const key = makeKey(country, fDate, sku, desc, month, year); if (existingKeys.has(key)) continue; if (maxExistingDate && fDate <= maxExistingDate) continue; output.push(newRow); // Append only genuinely new rows
Google Apps Script
SIPP Orders Auto-Sync
Too Good To Go · Barcelona

Apps Script that monitors a Drive folder, identifies the most recently updated Google Sheets file, extracts order data with proper date normalisation (converting Google Sheets serial dates to DD/MM/YYYY format), and writes it directly into the SIPP planning tool — keeping sales, inventory, and production data in sync automatically.

Auto
File detection
Live
SIPP sync
0
Manual exports
// Auto-detect newest file, normalise dates, write to SIPP const dateSerial = Math.floor( (Date.UTC(d.getFullYear(), d.getMonth(), d.getDate()) - Date.UTC(1899, 11, 30)) / 86400000); targetSheet.setNumberFormat("dd/MM/yyyy");
Le Wagon Bootcamp · Time Series

ARIMA Demand Forecasting

Two-part time series forecasting project implementing ARIMA and SARIMA models from scratch in Python. Covers stationarity testing (Augmented Dickey-Fuller), ACF/PACF analysis to identify AR and MA orders, differencing, and model evaluation. Directly applicable to demand planning — the same statistical foundation behind SAP IBP's statistical forecasting engine.

GitHub → ARIMA 101 →
Python ARIMA · SARIMA statsmodels Time Series Demand Forecasting Le Wagon
Le Wagon Bootcamp · MLOps & Cloud

Train in the Cloud — MLOps Pipeline

End-to-end MLOps project training a machine learning model on Google Cloud Platform. Covers containerising a model with Docker, pushing to Google Container Registry, training on cloud VMs, storing artefacts in Google Cloud Storage, and serving predictions via API. Shows ability to take a model from notebook to production infrastructure.

GitHub →
Python Docker Google Cloud MLOps Le Wagon
Le Wagon Bootcamp · API Development

FastAPI — ML Model Deployment

Built and deployed a machine learning model as a REST API using FastAPI. Covers creating prediction endpoints, input validation with Pydantic, and connecting a trained model to a live API that returns predictions in real time. The foundation for making any ML model accessible to other tools and teams.

GitHub →
Python FastAPI Pydantic REST API Model Deployment Le Wagon
Le Wagon Bootcamp · NLP & Transformers

Transformer Fine-Tuning

Fine-tuned a pre-trained transformer model (BERT/DistilBERT) on a custom classification task using Hugging Face. Covers tokenisation, transfer learning, training loops, and evaluating a fine-tuned NLP model. Demonstrates ability to work with state-of-the-art LLM infrastructure — directly relevant to AI-assisted supply chain workflows.

GitHub →
Python Hugging Face BERT Transfer Learning NLP Le Wagon
Le Wagon Bootcamp · Recommendation Systems

Spotify Music Recommendation Engine

Built a content-based and collaborative filtering recommendation system using Spotify track data. Covers feature engineering on audio features (tempo, energy, danceability), cosine similarity, and user-based recommendations. The same techniques apply to product recommendation and demand sensing in retail supply chains.

GitHub →
Python scikit-learn Cosine Similarity Recommendation Systems Le Wagon
Le Wagon Bootcamp · Deep Learning

ECG Classification — Medical Time Series

Deep learning model classifying electrocardiogram (ECG) signals into cardiac conditions using CNN and RNN architectures. A challenging time series classification task on real medical data — the same sequence modelling techniques apply directly to anomaly detection and pattern recognition in demand and sensor data.

GitHub →
Python TensorFlow CNN · RNN Time Series Classification Deep Learning Le Wagon
Le Wagon · Data Science & AI Bootcamp

Bootcamp Projects

Two projects built during the Le Wagon Data Science & AI Bootcamp (Mar–Sept 2025) — one solo, one as part of a 3-person team. Both are open source on GitHub.

Le Wagon Bootcamp · Solo Project

Inventory Tracker — Streamlit App

A live interactive web application built with Python and Streamlit, using st.data_editor to read and modify a SQLite inventory database directly in the browser. Users can add, edit, and delete inventory records in real time — no SQL knowledge required.

Demonstrates full-stack data app development: Python backend, SQLite database, and a deployed interactive UI — all in a single script.

▶ Live App GitHub →
Python Streamlit SQLite Web App Le Wagon
Le Wagon Bootcamp · Group Project (3 contributors)

Industrial Anomaly Detector — Predictive Maintenance ML

500,000 sensor readings. 6 models. 7-day failure prediction.

Dual-task predictive maintenance system that answers two questions: "Will this machine fail in the next 7 days?" (Classification) and "How many useful life days remain?" (Regression). Trained on 500K sensor readings across Boilers, Furnaces, and Heat Exchangers. Business case: traditional reactive and scheduled maintenance both waste money — a Boiler failure alone can cost €112k–360k in downtime, replacement, and indirect costs.

6 models compared with GridSearchCV — best hit 96% accuracy, 96% recall on failures. Impact: reduces downtime from weeks to hours, avoids unnecessary replacements saving hundreds of thousands of euros per asset. Deployed as MaintAIn 4.0, a 4-section Streamlit app with live dashboard, sensor-based prediction, bring-your-own-CSV training mode with SHAP explainability, and team page.

Built a full ML pipeline: data exploration, class imbalance handling with SMOTE oversampling, feature engineering, and model comparison across Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Decision Tree, XGBoost, and Gradient Boosting. Deployed as a Streamlit app with a demo video.

GitHub →

Planning Infrastructure

Excel Planning Tools

Beyond dashboards and code, I design the operational planning tools that teams use every day — rolling forecast models, integrated S&OP trackers, inventory monitors, and production frameworks.

Too Good To Go · Barcelona

Rolling Forecast Tool

Multi-market rolling forecast model covering the full demand funnel — from user metrics through to orders, parcels, and revenue. Designed for the monthly S&OP cycle with scenario planning, seasonality adjustments, and actuals vs forecast variance tracking built in. Fed automatically by the Google Apps Script pipelines.

ExcelGoogle Apps ScriptDemand PlanningS&OPMulti-market
Too Good To Go · Barcelona

SIPP — Sales, Inventory & Production Planning

Integrated planning tool connecting sales forecasts to inventory positions and production requirements. Consolidates demand signals, stock levels, and production capacity into a single planning view — auto-updated via Apps Script and visualised in Looker Studio for cross-functional visibility.

ExcelLooker StudioApps ScriptInventory PlanningProduction Planning
Too Good To Go · Barcelona

Push Notification (PN) Analysis Tool

Push notification performance analysis tool linking marketing activity to demand outcomes. Tracks open rates, conversion rates, and sales uplift by market — enabling the team to quantify demand impact of PN campaigns and refine commercial assumptions in the monthly forecast.

ExcelMarketing AnalyticsDemand DriversConversion Analysis
Too Good To Go · Barcelona

Production Analysis Framework

Production planning and analysis tool tracking manufacturing output against demand requirements. Monitors production efficiency, flags capacity gaps ahead of peak periods, and feeds into the S&OP supply review — ensuring alignment between demand forecasts and production capacity across markets.

ExcelProduction PlanningCapacity ManagementS&OP
